detailed_analysis: headers.py is a small, cohesive adapter-layer helper (136 lines, 4 functions, 0 classes) that translates HTTP-header carriers between the canonical MCP-server document shape and the per-dialect on-disk JSON spelling. It is well within the project's size limits (functions <=40 lines, module <=300 lines) and reads cleanly: the two private extractors are documented with intent-revealing docstrings, names are two-word/role-based, and the module imports its env-reference and dialect collaborators rather than re-implementing them (good DRY/SRP). No security smells of the C1-C5 class: there are no hardcoded secrets, no injection vectors, no silent except-swallowing, no mutable default arguments, and no no-op bodies. The secret policy (NFR-15) is correctly applied one layer out in parse.py (line 75) and render.py (line 60) at the artifact-egress boundary, so this helper operating on already-policy-screened data is consistent with goal 6 ('evaluated by the sync core at every egress boundary ... not per adapter'). The few findings are MINOR/SMELL: a primitive-obsession-adjacent reliance on untyped dict[str,Any] carriers, repeated magic string literals ('canonical', 'Authorization', 'Bearer'), no fail-fast assertion on the dialect/obj invariants at entry, and a borderline parameter count on render_http_headers. None of these cap an axis below 0.75. The module conforms to the architecture: it is an Interface-Adapter-layer (Layer 3) helper that imports only inward (mcp_secret_policy, _helpers, dialect) and never references a concrete tool name, upholding the Dependency Rule and Goal 5's stable-abstraction rule.

findings.evidence:
format_env_reference(str(env_name), style="canonical")
...
f"Bearer {format_env_reference(env_name, style='canonical')}"
...
headers["Authorization"] = ...
authorization = headers.get("Authorization")
headers.pop("Authorization", None)
findings.problem: The literals "canonical" (lines 44, 52), "Authorization" (lines 51, 94, 105), and the "Bearer " prefix (line 52) are repeated string constants embedded inline rather than named constants with intent. "Authorization" in particular is the same protocol header name written three times across two functions; a typo in any one drifts silently. AGENTS.md s9 calls for naming such values as constants. (The "canonical" style token is a protocol-defined enum value of format_env_reference, so it is the least severe.)

findings.evidence:
def render_http_headers(
    canonical: dict[str, Any],
    obj: dict[str, Any],
    dialect: McpServerDialect,
    tool_only: dict[str, Any],
    prior_obj: dict[str, Any],
) -> None:
findings.problem: render_http_headers takes 5 positional dict/dialect parameters (canonical, obj, dialect, tool_only, prior_obj). This sits exactly at Fowler's threshold (>5 is the flag) so it is not a violation, but four of the five are untyped dict[str, Any] bags that travel together as a render-context clump (also passed in pairs to the two private extractors). A render-context dataclass would clarify intent and reduce the data-clump shape. Reported as a SMELL, not a defect.

Both public functions accept and return raw dict[str, Any] carriers with an implicit schema (header-name -> value, env-header maps, the slot object) and immediately index dialect-named keys out of them. This is the documented house style for the mcp_server_io slot pipeline and the values are str-keyed by construction, so it is a SMELL rather than a MAJOR -- but the untyped bags mean the header schema is enforced only by str() coercion (lines 43, 50, 104, 128, 135), not by a typed boundary.

Neither public function asserts its entry invariants (AGENTS.md s8 'validate at boundaries'). headers_from_slot indexes obj[headers_field] trusting first_present, and relies on the delegated as_mapping helper to raise on a non-dict carrier; render_http_headers guards raw_headers with an isinstance check (line 65, good) but does not assert obj/tool_only/prior_obj are dicts. This is mild because malformed input surfaces a ValueError from as_mapping rather than a silent wrong result -- so it fails loud enough -- but the boundary is implicit, not explicit. SMELL.

verdict_summary: Clean, cohesive Layer-3 header-translation helper; only MINOR/SMELL maintainability nits (magic strings, untyped dict carriers); conforms to NFR-15, FR-09, and the Dependency Rule.
